name: Eurosky
tagline: European-hosted AT Protocol accounts that act as your PDS and work across Bluesky, Flashes, Tangled, and other Atmosphere apps.
fullDescription: Eurosky offers European-hosted eurosky.social accounts built on the AT Protocol. Each account acts as a personal data server (PDS), so your identity, posts, and social graph stay portable across apps while remaining governed under European law and infrastructure.
classificationReason: Eurosky provides hosted AT Protocol accounts whose eurosky.social identity acts as a personal data server, so it belongs in the protocol/PDS category.

name: Taproot
tagline: Comprehensive AT Protocol explorer for repositories, records, PDSes, and relays.
fullDescription: Taproot is a comprehensive AT Protocol explorer for browsing repositories, records, collections, PDSes, relays, and other infrastructure across the network. It also lets you look up handles, DIDs, and full at:// URIs from a mobile-friendly web app.
classificationReason: Taproot is a protocol-wide explorer for repositories, records, collections, PDSes, relays, and at:// URIs, so it fits the protocol/data-explorer category.

name: atp.tools
tagline: AT Protocol explorer for users, repositories, collections, and related tooling.
fullDescription: atp.tools is a web-based AT Protocol explorer for searching users, repositories, and collections, with additional utilities like Jetstream and Counter for inspecting network activity and protocol data. It also supports logging in to explore account-scoped views from the browser.
classificationReason: atp.tools is a protocol-wide explorer and utility suite for browsing AT Protocol identities, repos, collections, and infrastructure data, so it fits the protocol/data-explorer category.

name: Selfhosted.social
tagline: Open ATProto PDS with no invite code and nightly backups.
fullDescription: Selfhosted.social is an open ATProto personal data server that welcomes new signups and migrations without an invite code. It is hosted on DigitalOcean in Atlanta and includes nightly server backups plus per-account backups through PDS MOOver.
classificationReason: Selfhosted.social provides hosted AT Protocol accounts on its own personal data server, so it belongs in the protocol/pds category.

name: PDS.RIP
tagline: Ephemeral sandbox PDS for AT Protocol testing and experimentation.
fullDescription: PDS.RIP is a public sandbox personal data server for the AT Protocol with open registration and captcha protection for testing, prototyping, and experimentation. Accounts and all associated data are ephemeral and are permanently deleted after 7 days, so it is best suited for temporary use rather than primary hosting.
classificationReason: PDS.RIP provides hosted AT Protocol accounts on its own personal data server, so it belongs in the protocol/pds category.

name: @atproto/lex
tagline: Type-safe Lexicon tooling for AT Protocol data and schemas.
fullDescription: @atproto/lex is a preview package from the Bluesky atproto monorepo for working with AT Protocol Lexicon schemas. It can fetch and manage Lexicon documents, generate TypeScript validators and schema utilities, and provide compile-time and runtime type safety for AT Protocol data structures and XRPC usage.
classificationReason: @atproto/lex is purpose-built for authoring, fetching, generating, and validating AT Protocol Lexicon schemas, so it fits the protocol/lexicon category.

name: tap
tagline: AT Protocol sync utility for firehose ingestion, backfill, and filtered delivery.
fullDescription: tap is an atproto sync utility from the Indigo repo that handles firehose connection, verification, historical backfill, filtering, and event delivery for the repos and collections you care about. It is designed to simplify AT Protocol sync workloads by exposing ordered JSON events over WebSocket or webhook instead of requiring consumers to implement binary parsing and signature validation themselves.
classificationReason: tap is purpose-built for syncing AT Protocol repo data from the firehose with validation, backfill, and filtered delivery, so it fits the protocol/sync category.

name: @atproto/tap
tagline: TypeScript client library for connecting to Tap sync servers.
fullDescription: @atproto/tap is a TypeScript client library for Tap, the AT Protocol sync utility that handles firehose connections, cryptographic verification, backfill, and filtering. The library provides a typed client, channel handling, indexer helpers, webhook parsing utilities, and schema-aware event handling for consuming ordered JSON events from a Tap instance.
classificationReason: @atproto/tap is a developer library specifically for consuming and handling Tap sync events from AT Protocol infrastructure, so it fits the protocol/sync category.

name: HappyView
tagline: Schema-first AppView platform for the AT Protocol with built-in sync and OAuth.
fullDescription: HappyView is a schema-first platform for building AT Protocol AppViews by uploading lexicon schemas and getting XRPC endpoints, OAuth, real-time sync, historical backfill, indexing, and admin tooling without writing server boilerplate. It also supports Lua scripting, index hooks, PostgreSQL-backed indexing, and runtime lexicon updates for shipping protocol-native apps faster.
classificationReason: HappyView provides managed AppView infrastructure with XRPC endpoints, sync, indexing, and OAuth for AT Protocol applications, so it fits the protocol/appview category.

name: Quickslice
tagline: Auto-indexing AppView platform with GraphQL and OAuth for AT Protocol records.
fullDescription: Quickslice is a quick way to spin up an AT Protocol AppView by importing lexicon schemas and getting a GraphQL API, OAuth authentication, real-time network sync, indexing, and joins across record types without building backend infrastructure first. It supports SQLite or Postgres, writes records back to the user's PDS, and includes a web-based playground and admin UI for developing protocol-native apps.
classificationReason: Quickslice provides managed AppView-style infrastructure for indexing AT Protocol records and exposing app APIs, so it fits the protocol/appview category.

name: lexicon.js
tagline: Plain JavaScript validator for AT Protocol Lexicon schemas.
fullDescription: lexicon.js is a plain JavaScript validator for AT Protocol Lexicon schemas, published on npm as @bigmoves/lexicon. It validates lexicon schema definitions and records against those schemas, and is aimed at lightweight spec-compliant lexicon work without depending on the broader official toolchain.
classificationReason: lexicon.js is purpose-built for validating AT Protocol Lexicon schemas and records, so it fits the protocol/lexicon category.

name: lex-gql
tagline: Generate a complete GraphQL API from AT Protocol lexicons.
fullDescription: lex-gql is a library for generating a complete GraphQL API from AT Protocol lexicons. It provides relay-style pagination, automatic joins between collections, filtering, sorting, aggregations, CRUD mutations, subscriptions, and batched join resolution for building protocol-native data APIs on top of lexicon-defined records.
classificationReason: lex-gql is a reusable developer library for building GraphQL APIs from AT Protocol lexicons, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: pds.js
tagline: Minimal AT Protocol Personal Data Server written in JavaScript.
fullDescription: pds.js is an experimental, minimal AT Protocol Personal Data Server implementation written in JavaScript for self-hosting and development. It includes core repo and sync endpoints, auth and OAuth flows, AppView proxying, blob storage, and deployment options for Node.js, Deno, and Cloudflare Workers through a modular multi-package architecture.
classificationReason: pds.js is a self-hostable implementation of an AT Protocol personal data server, so it belongs in the protocol/pds category.

name: goat
tagline: General-purpose Go CLI for exploring and administering the AT Protocol.
fullDescription: goat is a general-purpose Go CLI tool for the AT Protocol, sort of like curl for the network. It can fetch at:// URIs, inspect identities, monitor the firehose, migrate accounts, administer PDS instances, make raw XRPC calls, and support Lexicon development workflows such as pulling, linting, diffing, and publishing schemas.
classificationReason: goat is a command-line developer tool for interacting with many parts of the AT Protocol stack, so a dedicated protocol/cli category is cleaner than putting it in data-explorer.

name: atcute
tagline: Lightweight TypeScript packages for AT Protocol and Bluesky.
fullDescription: atcute is a collection of lightweight TypeScript packages for the AT Protocol, covering XRPC clients, firehose and Jetstream clients, Tap clients, XRPC servers, OAuth helpers, lexicon tooling, identity resolution, core codecs, and Bluesky-specific utilities. It is designed as a modular toolkit so applications can compose only the protocol pieces they need instead of adopting a monolithic SDK.
classificationReason: atcute is a reusable developer toolkit made up of many protocol libraries for AT Protocol clients, servers, lexicons, identity, and utilities, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: Inlay
tagline: Composable blocks for AT Protocol.
fullDescription: Inlay is a standalone AT Protocol app for composing reusable blocks from live protocol data such as profiles, posts, replies, media, playlists, and raw records. You can point it at an at:// URI, combine presentation components, and build shareable layouts around AT Protocol content instead of treating posts and profiles as static screenshots.
classificationReason: Inlay is a standalone app centered on composing and sharing reusable blocks built from AT Protocol profiles and records, so it fits best as its own app ecosystem entry at apps/inlay rather than under a generic Bluesky subcategory.

name: atproto
tagline: Autogenerated, type-hinted AT Protocol SDK for Python.
fullDescription: atproto is a Python SDK for the AT Protocol that provides autogenerated XRPC clients, typed models, sync and async APIs, firehose streaming support, identity resolvers, lexicon tooling, and lower-level utilities for AT URIs, CIDs, CAR files, DAG-CBOR, signatures, and DID documents. It is designed as a reusable developer library so Python applications can interact with Bluesky and broader AT Protocol services through both high-level helpers and the underlying generated protocol methods.
classificationReason: atproto is a reusable Python SDK and supporting library suite for working with AT Protocol clients, models, firehose streams, identities, lexicons, and core protocol data types, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: rsky
tagline: Rust implementation of the AT Protocol for community safety and self-governance.
fullDescription: rsky is a Rust implementation of the AT Protocol that aims to provide both reusable crates and deployable services across the stack. The workspace includes libraries for crypto, identity, lexicons, syntax, and repo storage, alongside components such as a PDS, relay, feed generator, firehose consumers, labeler, repository explorer, and appview indexer, with an emphasis on community safety and self-governance.
classificationReason: rsky is primarily a reusable Rust implementation and crate ecosystem for AT Protocol primitives and infrastructure components, so it fits the protocol/libraries category even though the monorepo also includes deployable services.

name: AT Protocol Rust Crates
tagline: Rust workspace of crates for building AT Protocol applications.
fullDescription: AT Protocol Rust Crates is a Rust workspace of reusable crates for building AT Protocol applications and infrastructure. It provides building blocks for content-addressed data, identity management, repository operations, record signing, lexicon validation, OAuth flows, XRPC clients and services, real-time event processing, rich text utilities, and developer tooling, with components that can be composed into clients, PDSes, AIPs, event processors, and repository tools.
classificationReason: AT Protocol Rust Crates is a reusable Rust crate workspace for many AT Protocol primitives and developer building blocks, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: atproto.dart
tagline: Production-ready AT Protocol and Bluesky SDK for Flutter and Dart.
fullDescription: atproto.dart is a comprehensive Dart and Flutter SDK for building AT Protocol and Bluesky applications, bots, and services. It includes core protocol primitives such as DIDs, NSIDs, AT URIs, Lexicons, and XRPC, along with package libraries for Bluesky clients, OAuth DPoP authentication, Firehose streaming, moderation tooling, service development, rich text handling, and related developer workflows across mobile, web, and backend environments.
classificationReason: atproto.dart is a reusable SDK and package ecosystem for Dart and Flutter developers building AT Protocol clients, services, and tooling, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: ATProtoKit
tagline: Straightforward Swift library for using the AT Protocol and Bluesky.
fullDescription: ATProtoKit is a Swift package for using the AT Protocol and Bluesky with an API shape designed to feel native to Swift. It focuses primarily on client-side AT Protocol usage, combining type-safe API and XRPC access for bots, server apps, and user-facing clients, with support for Apple platforms, Linux, and other Swift environments.
classificationReason: ATProtoKit is a reusable Swift library for AT Protocol and Bluesky client development, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: swift-atproto
tagline: Swift library and code generator for building AT Protocol clients and servers.
fullDescription: swift-atproto is a Swift package for working with the AT Protocol that combines a reusable library with plugin-based code generation from lexicon schemas. It supports Swift Package Manager and CocoaPods, can generate client and server code from configured AT Protocol lexicons, and is intended to help Swift developers build AT Protocol integrations with typed APIs instead of hand-authoring the protocol surface themselves.
classificationReason: swift-atproto is a reusable Swift package and code generation library for AT Protocol client and server development, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: idunno.Bluesky
tagline: .NET SDK for Bluesky and the AT Protocol.
fullDescription: idunno.Bluesky is a .NET 8, 9, and 10 library and SDK for Bluesky and the AT Protocol. It provides high-level support for creating and deleting posts, media uploads, timelines, feeds, notifications, follows, blocks, bookmarks, direct messages, handle or OAuth authentication, Jetstream consumption, and automatic session management with background token refreshes for .NET applications.
classificationReason: idunno.Bluesky is a reusable .NET SDK for integrating Bluesky and AT Protocol capabilities into applications, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: ATProto Ruby SDK
tagline: Ruby gems for interacting with and building on Bluesky and the AT Protocol.
fullDescription: ATProto Ruby SDK is a collection of Ruby gems for working with Bluesky APIs and building on the AT Protocol. It brings together focused libraries for XRPC requests, firehose and Jetstream streaming, DID and handle resolution, feed generator hosting, and Tap-based repo import, giving Ruby developers battle-tested building blocks instead of a single monolithic SDK.
classificationReason: ATProto Ruby SDK is a reusable set of Ruby libraries for multiple parts of the AT Protocol stack, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: zat
tagline: AT Protocol building blocks for Zig.
fullDescription: zat is a Zig library of AT Protocol building blocks that covers identifier parsing, handle and DID resolution, DAG-CBOR and CAR codecs, Merkle Search Tree operations, crypto and key handling, full repo verification, firehose and Jetstream clients, XRPC requests, and JSON extraction helpers. It is designed as a reusable low-level toolkit for building AT Protocol applications, validators, and infrastructure in Zig rather than a single high-level monolithic client.
classificationReason: zat is a reusable Zig toolkit for many AT Protocol primitives and infrastructure concerns, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: atex
tagline: Elixir toolkit for the AT Protocol.
fullDescription: atex is an Elixir toolkit for the AT Protocol that bundles string primitives, identity resolution, lexicon-to-struct code generation, OAuth, XRPC clients, repository reading and manipulation, service auth, PLC access, and an XRPC server router. It is designed as a reusable library for building AT Protocol clients and services in Elixir rather than a single app-specific SDK.
classificationReason: atex is a reusable Elixir toolkit for multiple AT Protocol primitives, clients, and server-side building blocks, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: Libphpsky
tagline: Statically typed PHP library for Bluesky and the AT Protocol.
fullDescription: Libphpsky is a PHP library for interacting with Bluesky and the AT Protocol using generated types, queries, and procedures derived from the protocol schema. It includes built-in authorization with automatic session refresh, a meta client for reaching any endpoint, async support via Amp HTTP clients, WebSocket subscriptions, query caching, and custom lexicon support for PHP applications and frameworks.
classificationReason: Libphpsky is a reusable PHP SDK for AT Protocol and Bluesky integrations, so it fits the protocol/libraries category.

name: Jetstream
tagline: Simplified JSON event stream for the AT Protocol.
fullDescription: Jetstream is a streaming service that consumes the AT Protocol com.atproto.sync.subscribeRepos firehose and converts CBOR-encoded MST data into lightweight JSON events that are easier to consume with standard tooling. It supports collection and DID filtering, cursor-based replay, zstd compression, subscriber-sourced filter updates, self-hosting, and public Bluesky-operated instances for developers who want sync data without implementing the full binary sync protocol.
classificationReason: Jetstream is purpose-built for consuming and republishing AT Protocol sync data as a simpler event stream, so it fits the protocol/sync category.

name: Lexicon Garden
tagline: Browse and resolve ATProtocol lexicon schemas.
fullDescription: Lexicon Garden is a discovery platform for ATProtocol lexicon schemas that indexes schemas published to the network and makes them easier to browse, search, validate, and understand. It also provides tools for schema resolution, validation, transmogrification, scope building, contributed examples, and MCP integration for developers working with lexicon-defined APIs and records.
classificationReason: Lexicon Garden is specifically focused on discovering, browsing, resolving, and validating AT Protocol lexicon schemas, so it fits the protocol/lexicon category.

name: UFOs
tagline: Explore AT Protocol lexicons and collection activity.
fullDescription: UFOs is a web app for exploring AT Protocol lexicons through the collections currently seen across the network. It lets developers browse collections, inspect sample records, and track timeseries statistics for each collection, making it easier to understand how lexicon-defined records are actually used in the ATmosphere.
classificationReason: UFOs is specifically focused on browsing AT Protocol collections and understanding lexicon-defined record types through samples and activity statistics, so it fits the protocol/lexicon category.

name: BskyCharts
tagline: Public Bluesky charts for activity, growth, and network health.
fullDescription: BskyCharts is a public monitoring dashboard for Bluesky activity and related network signals, published as a set of continuously refreshed charts. It tracks metrics such as overall activity, embed usage, labels added, operation rates, post languages, user growth, bsky.network PDS status, and PLC activity across day, week, month, and year views.
classificationReason: BskyCharts is a standalone Bluesky-focused web dashboard for monitoring activity, growth, and operational metrics, so it fits the apps/bluesky/analytics category.

name: Puzzmo
tagline: The new place for thoughtful puzzles.
fullDescription: Puzzmo is a web-based home for thoughtful puzzles that reimagines familiar daily formats while also publishing modern puzzle games and original experiments. Its lineup includes refreshed staples like the daily crossword alongside games such as Really Bad Chess and SpellTower, plus new puzzle concepts built as part of the broader Puzzmo ecosystem.
classificationReason: Puzzmo is a standalone consumer puzzle platform with its own recognizable product ecosystem, so it fits best as an app-root entry at apps/puzzmo rather than under a generic Bluesky or protocol category.

name: teal.fm
tagline: Your music, beautifully tracked. All yours (soon).
fullDescription: teal.fm is an upcoming music tracking and social discovery app built on ATProto. It is designed to sync your listening history across platforms like Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, and more, while also helping you own your music data, discover tracks through shared taste, and generate beautiful cards for sharing your top artists and songs.
classificationReason: teal.fm is a standalone music product with its own app ecosystem identity, combining cross-platform listening history, social discovery, and sharing features, so it fits best as an app-root entry at apps/teal-fm.

name: Standard.site
tagline: Shared lexicons for long-form publishing on AT Protocol.
fullDescription: Standard.site is a community-maintained schema project for long-form publishing on AT Protocol. It defines shared lexicons for publications, documents, and subscriptions so apps and platforms can make long-form content easier to discover, verify, index, and move across the ATmosphere without each implementation inventing incompatible schemas.
classificationReason: Standard.site is specifically focused on defining and coordinating shared AT Protocol lexicons for long-form publishing, so it fits the protocol/lexicon category.

name: ATGeo
tagline: Geographic place lexicon and venue lookup service for AT Protocol.
fullDescription: ATGeo provides a shared geographic place lexicon for AT Protocol applications along with a public gazetteer service that implements the spec. It defines how points of interest are represented and offers an XRPC API for searching and retrieving venue data from sources like OpenStreetMap, Overture Maps, and Foursquare so apps can use interoperable place records instead of inventing incompatible location models.
classificationReason: ATGeo is specifically focused on defining and serving a reusable AT Protocol place lexicon for applications, so it fits the protocol/lexicon category even though it also exposes a hosted lookup API.

name: Docs.surf
tagline: Aggregator for publications and documents on the open web.
fullDescription: Docs.surf is a Standard.site aggregator that pulls valid publications and documents into a single chronological feed. It acts like an ATProto-native publishing reader and discovery surface, collecting long-form documents from across the open web without requiring users to manually subscribe to each source one by one.
classificationReason: Docs.surf is a standalone reader and discovery app built around the Standard.site publishing ecosystem, so it fits best as an app-root entry at apps/docs-surf rather than as protocol infrastructure.

name: Bluefeed
tagline: Feed Builder & Viewer for Bluesky.
fullDescription: Bluefeed is a web app for building and viewing custom Bluesky feeds, helping users curate timeline experiences around specific topics and communities.
classificationReason: Bluefeed is purpose-built for creating and browsing custom Bluesky feeds, so it fits the Bluesky feed-generators category.

name: Skyblur
tagline: Post spoiler-protected Bluesky content with bracket-based blur markup.
fullDescription: Skyblur is an AT Protocol-compatible posting tool for Bluesky that lets you mask spoiler text by wrapping segments in square brackets before publishing. It posts directly to Bluesky with blurred placeholders in-feed while preserving a readable unblurred version in Skyblur, so people can opt in to reveal hidden content.
classificationReason: Skyblur helps users apply spoiler masking and content warnings to Bluesky posts, which is a moderation and safety workflow, so it fits apps/bluesky/moderation.

name: SensorThings on ATProto
tagline: Map and time-series explorer for environmental sensor records on AT Protocol.
fullDescription: SensorThings on ATProto is a web app for exploring Irish environmental monitoring data published as signed AT Protocol records. It visualizes station locations and datastream history for water levels, ocean conditions, air quality, and weather while exposing the underlying OGC SensorThings-style entity model and AT-URI-linked records.
classificationReason: The product is a user-facing geospatial and time-series exploration app centered on station locations and place-based sensor datastreams, so it fits an apps/location category.

name: mapped.at
tagline: Trails and activities, built on the open AT protocol.
fullDescription: mapped.at is an AT Protocol app for discovering community trails, logging outdoor activities, and connecting with other adventurers on an open, decentralized network.
classificationReason: mapped.at centers on trail discovery and activity logging with social features, making it a location-focused AT Protocol app.

name: GainForest
tagline: Protecting and restoring Earth's forests with transparent conservation data.
fullDescription: GainForest is a decentralized platform for forest conservation that combines satellite monitoring, AI models, and transparent funding flows to support restoration and stewardship projects. The app visualizes project locations and environmental signals while helping communities and supporters track outcomes over time.
classificationReason: GainForest is map- and place-centric, presenting conservation projects through geospatial context and location-based monitoring, so it fits apps/location.

name: FreeMix
tagline: Your music. Your stems. Your rules.
fullDescription: FreeMix is a decentralized music platform built on AT Protocol for sharing, discovering, and remixing tracks, stems, and samples with clear open licensing. It supports producer-focused workflows like stem uploads, remix trees, activity feeds, and explicit license controls so artists can collaborate while keeping rights transparent.
classificationReason: FreeMix is a standalone AT Protocol-native music product focused on uploading, licensing, and remixing tracks and stems, so it fits best as an app-root entry at apps/freemix with a music domain.

name: Hyperboards
tagline: See who built what, beautifully.
fullDescription: Hyperboards is an ATProto-native app for turning signed contributor records into live, embeddable treemap boards. It helps open-source teams, climate projects, DAOs, and collectives make contribution data visible with portable, verifiable attribution that updates continuously.
classificationReason: Hyperboards is a builder-focused ATProto tool for recording and visualizing contribution data in embeddable boards, making it a strong fit for the Bluesky tooling category and the Developers app tag.

name: dinger.tv
tagline: Live Home Run Control Center for Bluesky.
fullDescription: dinger.tv is a sports feed app for Bluesky that tracks MLB home run posts in real time. You can follow every home run by default, or sign in with Bluesky to favorite specific players and teams, save those picks, and narrow the feed to exactly the hitters and clubs you care about.
classificationReason: dinger.tv is a user-facing sports app with its own product identity that helps people customize and preview a Bluesky home run feed, so it fits best as an app-root listing at apps/dinger-tv with a sports vertical.

name: Passing Reads
tagline: Share your old books with new friends and discover stories nearby.
fullDescription: Passing Reads is a local book-sharing app where people leave physical books for others to find, read, and pass on. It helps readers discover nearby dropped books, track community activity, and print labels so books can keep moving from one reader to the next.
classificationReason: Passing Reads centers on finding and sharing physical books by nearby location, so it best fits apps/location with a books vertical.

name: ATBuddy
tagline: Your AT Protocol Companion
fullDescription: ATBuddy is a decentralized virtual-pet companion built on AT Protocol. You hatch, feed, and care for your buddy through the app or by mentioning @atbuddy.app on Bluesky for commands like stats, user lookup, achievements, and help, with buddy state stored in your own PDS account rather than in a centralized backend.
classificationReason: ATBuddy is a standalone consumer game experience built around an AT Protocol identity with gameplay interactions in-app and via Bluesky, so it fits best as an app-root listing with games domain at apps/atbuddy.

name: Registry.Blue
tagline: A wishlist and registry app, powered by the AT Protocol.
fullDescription: Registry.Blue is an AT Protocol-native wishlist and registry app where people can create, share, and manage gift wishlists with Bluesky login.
classificationReason: Registry.Blue is a user-facing social wishlist product with its own brand and workflow, so it fits best as an app-root listing at apps/registry-blue while also mapping to the Social app tag.

name: Botwatch
tagline: Fight bots with shared bot and trust scores.
fullDescription: Botwatch is a trust and anti-bot app where people flag suspected bot accounts, verify humans, and publish trust scores others can evaluate. It is built to make moderation and reputation signals transparent and verifiable so communities can decide who to trust.
classificationReason: Botwatch provides technical trust and bot-scoring infrastructure for Bluesky communities, so it fits the Bluesky tooling category and the Developer app tag.

name: Ptah Protocol
tagline: Open infrastructure for creative authorship on the AT Protocol.
fullDescription: Ptah Protocol is an open lexicon suite for creative world-building on AT Protocol, with fifteen record types for authorship, attribution, lineage, rights, and versioned creative objects across media. It publishes production schemas under world.ptah.* so any AppView or client can index and render interoperable records without a central platform owner.
classificationReason: Ptah Protocol defines and publishes interoperable AT Protocol lexicon schemas under world.ptah.* for modeling creative records, so it fits the protocol/lexicon category.

name: ATCR
tagline: Push and pull Docker images on the AT Protocol.
fullDescription: ATCR is a distributed container registry for AT Protocol that lets developers push and pull OCI-compatible Docker images with standard Docker commands while browsing and discovering public registries.
classificationReason: ATCR provides developer infrastructure for building and deploying AT Protocol services using OCI container workflows.

name: Markpub.at
tagline: ATProto lexicons for portable Markdown publishing.
fullDescription: Markpub.at publishes the at.markpub.markdown lexicon family for embedding Markdown in AT Protocol records, including metadata for flavor, rendering rules, facets, and optional front matter/lenses.
classificationReason: Defines reusable ATProto lexicon schemas for representing and transporting Markdown content across apps.

name: Starrysky
tagline: AT Protocol based streaks and achievements.
fullDescription: Starrysky is a productivity app built on AT Protocol that helps users maintain daily check-in streaks and track achievements tied to their social identity.
classificationReason: Starrysky is a standalone AT Protocol app focused on daily habits, streak tracking, and achievement progress, which maps best to productivity.

name: lore.farm
tagline: Collectivized canon for fictional worlds.
fullDescription: lore.farm is an AT Protocol indexer for collaborative worldbuilding that tracks lore and canon declarations as verifiable records across Bluesky and the ATmosphere, helping communities build collectively owned fictional worlds.
classificationReason: lore.farm is a user-facing collaborative storytelling product built on AT Protocol where communities create and curate shared canon, so it fits an apps/social classification.

name: atpr.to
tagline: AT Protocol URL shortener — links stored in your own PDS.
fullDescription: atpr.to is an AT Protocol URL shortener that lets users create, manage, and search short links tied to their handle or DID, with QR exports and JSON API access.
classificationReason: atpr.to provides AT Protocol-native link management and API tooling useful to developers and technical operators, fitting the developers app category.

name: AtChef
tagline: Create and share recipes on AT Protocol.
fullDescription: AtChef is an AT Protocol-native recipe sharing app where people publish and discover dishes through Bluesky identity, making it easy to collect cooking ideas from the community.
classificationReason: AtChef is a user-facing recipe sharing app built on AT Protocol and centered on community discovery, so it fits the social apps category while mapping to the food app tag.

name: blebbit
tagline: A thoughtful blend of Discord + Reddit + Groups.
fullDescription: blebbit is an AT Protocol community spaces app that combines forum-style communities, chats, and feeds into one social experience.
classificationReason: blebbit is a user-facing AT Protocol app for communities, chats, and feeds, so it fits the social apps category.

name: BridgeBeats
tagline: Share music links across streaming services.
fullDescription: BridgeBeats is a music link conversion service that matches tracks and albums across Apple Music, Spotify, and Tidal using ISRC/UPC and metadata, then generates shareable cross-platform links for listeners, Discord communities, and developer integrations.
classificationReason: BridgeBeats is a user-facing music utility focused on cross-platform track and album link matching and sharing, so it fits an apps/audio category.

name: bragsheet
tagline: Share the wins you're proud of.
fullDescription: bragsheet is a personal page app built on AT Protocol where you log in with Bluesky and curate accomplishments as a living record of what you have done. Entries are tied back to your Bluesky identity so your profile and reputation signals remain portable across the network rather than locked to a single platform.
classificationReason: bragsheet centers on handle-linked personal pages and accomplishment profiles for Bluesky users, making it a profile utility that fits apps/bluesky/tool while mapping to the Personal Page app tag.

name: Unthread
tagline: Draft and publish Bluesky threads in one place.
fullDescription: Unthread is a focused Bluesky client for writing thread-style posts, saving drafts, and publishing finished unthreads from a dedicated drafts-and-posts workflow.
classificationReason: Unthread is an end-user Bluesky client focused on composing, saving, and publishing thread-style posts.

name: WeListSkyBlue
tagline: Social Task Management App. Built on AT Protocol.
fullDescription: WeListSkyBlue is a social task management app built on AT Protocol that helps people organize tasks, track progress, and celebrate completions with social activity features.
classificationReason: WeListSkyBlue is a standalone AT Protocol application focused on collaborative task tracking and completion workflows, so it best fits a productivity app classification.

name: Lexicon Community
tagline: A working group to create community ATProtocol Lexicons.
fullDescription: Lexicon Community is a community working group that develops and coordinates AT Protocol lexicons, with open governance, discussion, and shared schema work across contributors.
classificationReason: Lexicon Community organizes and maintains shared AT Protocol lexicon definitions and related schema collaboration, so it fits the protocol/lexicon category.

name: Red Dwarf
tagline: Independent Bluesky client that does not depend on AppView servers.
fullDescription: Red Dwarf is an independent Bluesky web client that fetches records directly from each user's PDS and reconstructs relationships through backlinks, with optional AppView usage as an optimization layer.
classificationReason: A user-facing Bluesky client app for reading and interacting with AT Protocol content without requiring AppView infrastructure.

name: Mackinac
tagline: Your bridge to the Social Web.
fullDescription: Mackinac is a native iOS client for AT Protocol and Bluesky that helps people connect across the Fediverse and Atmosphere from one app.
rawCategoryHint: Native iOS AT Protocol and Bluesky client
classificationReason: Mackinac is a user-facing Bluesky/AT Protocol client app for browsing and posting on the social web, so it fits apps/bluesky/client.

name: Juttu
tagline: Comments that live on the open web
fullDescription: Juttu is an open-source comments widget built on AT Protocol where each comment is a real Bluesky post owned by the commenter, with no reader tracking and simple drop-in HTML integration.
classificationReason: Embeddable developer-facing comments widget that uses Bluesky reply threads as the comment backend.

name: dropb.at
tagline: Share and manage text, photos and other files on the AT Protocol.
fullDescription: dropb.at is an AT Protocol file-sharing app for uploading, organizing, and sharing text, photos, and other files while keeping control of your data and server choice.
classificationReason: dropb.at focuses on personal file management and sharing workflows, which fits a productivity-oriented app category.

name: Open Evnt
tagline: A modern data format for events.
fullDescription: Open Evnt is a modern, JSON-based event data format and schema toolkit that also defines AT Protocol lexicons for interoperable event publishing across apps and directories.
rawCategoryHint: Event data format with AT Protocol lexicons
classificationReason: Open Evnt defines and publishes AT Protocol event lexicons (including directory.evnt.event) and related schema tooling for developers, so it fits protocol/lexicon.

name: Anchor
tagline: Your personal location journal.
fullDescription: Anchor is a personal location journal app that lets you save meaningful places and revisit them over time. It uses Bluesky sign-in for account access and focuses on lightweight, map-centered memory keeping.
classificationReason: Anchor is a standalone location-focused product with its own brand and workflow for saving and revisiting places, so it fits best as an app-root listing at apps/drop-anchor.

name: deck belcher
tagline: Organize, build, and share decks on Atmosphere.
fullDescription: Deck Belcher is an atproto-powered deck companion for Atmosphere where players can organize decks, browse cards, and share builds with the community.
classificationReason: It is a consumer app for Atmosphere card game players with deck management and discovery features.

name: Chronosky
tagline: Schedule your Bluesky posts with ease.
fullDescription: Chronosky is a Bluesky scheduling app for planning, scheduling, and publishing posts when your audience is most engaged, with support for rich media, link card previews, flexible timezone-aware scheduling, and multilingual workflows.
classificationReason: Chronosky focuses on scheduling and publishing posts for Bluesky users, which fits the Bluesky scheduling apps category.

name: LinkedClaims
tagline: Decentralized verifiable claims on AT Protocol
fullDescription: LinkedClaims is an open standard and toolkit for publishing signed, verifiable claims about any URI-addressable subject using AT Protocol records, including support for endorsements, disputes, confidence ratings, and evidence attachments.
classificationReason: LinkedClaims provides developer-facing AT Protocol primitives, schema, and tooling for issuing and verifying signed claims, so it fits a developer-oriented apps category.

name: Stratos
tagline: Private permissioned data for ATprotocol
fullDescription: Stratos is a private, permissioned data layer for AT Protocol that stores boundary-scoped records off the firehose and serves them through protocol-native hydration.
classificationReason: Stratos provides permissioned shared-data infrastructure and integration docs for AT Protocol clients and operators.

name: Octosphere
tagline: Connecting open science with the social web
fullDescription: Octosphere bridges academic publishing and the social web by syncing publications from Octopus to the AT Protocol, helping researchers share work beyond traditional journals.
classificationReason: AT Protocol app focused on scientific publication discovery and sharing.

name: Hyperscan
tagline: Explore the Hypersphere across DIDs, handles, collections, and network activity.
fullDescription: Hyperscan is an ATProto data explorer from Hypercerts/GainForest for searching DIDs, handles, collections, and pages, with network analytics, live stream tooling, indexer management, and developer utilities.
classificationReason: Hyperscan is a protocol-level explorer for inspecting ATProto repos, collections, and network/indexer activity rather than an end-user social client.

name: Chavatar
tagline: Automatically rotate your Bluesky avatar.
fullDescription: Chavatar is a Bluesky utility that automatically rotates your profile avatar so you can keep your identity fresh without manual uploads. It is aimed at people who want lightweight profile personalization with a simple, focused workflow.
classificationReason: Chavatar is a user-facing Bluesky utility focused on profile avatar personalization and automation, so it fits the Bluesky tools category with an identity domain.

name: Sequoia
tagline: Publish evergreen content to the ATmosphere.
fullDescription: Sequoia is a simple CLI for turning existing static blog content into standard.site documents and publishing them to the AT Protocol. It supports OAuth-based auth, framework integrations, and local or CI workflows so developers can ship evergreen posts to the ATmosphere with minimal migration overhead.
classificationReason: Sequoia is a developer-focused CLI for publishing standard.site content to the AT Protocol, so it fits a dedicated app category for Sequoia ecosystem tooling.

name: bsky.md
tagline: Fetch public Bluesky profiles, posts, feeds, and search results as clean Markdown via simple HTTP endpoints.
fullDescription: bsky.md turns public Bluesky data into clean Markdown without authentication or API keys. It provides endpoints for profiles, posts, threads, feeds, likes, followers, following, search, links, and trending topics, making it useful for developers, automation, and coding agents.
classificationReason: Provides a developer-facing HTTP service for reading public Bluesky content as Markdown, which fits a Bluesky tooling utility.

name: atmo.garden
tagline: Communities on Bluesky, experimental.
fullDescription: atmo.garden is an experimental Bluesky community directory that helps people discover and browse communities across the ATmosphere by activity and freshness.
classificationReason: atmo.garden is a user-facing discovery app focused on Bluesky communities and social participation, so it fits apps/social.

name: My Location
tagline: Live GPS coordinates, address lookup, and location file tools in one web app.
fullDescription: My Location is a browser-based location utility that shows your current GPS coordinates, reverse-geocoded address context, and map-friendly location metadata. It also supports importing and exporting KML, GPX, and GeoJSON so users can move location tracks and points between mapping workflows and mobile tools.
classificationReason: The product is a standalone consumer location utility centered on coordinate lookup, address context, and geospatial file interchange, which fits apps/location.

name: Proto Instant Messenger
tagline: Private, peer-to-peer communication
fullDescription: Proto Instant Messenger is an atproto messaging app focused on private, peer-to-peer communication, with handle-based sign-in, customizable permissions, and multilingual support.
classificationReason: Messaging-first atproto app for direct peer-to-peer communication.

name: sidetrail
tagline: Trails worth walking.
fullDescription: Sidetrail is an AT Protocol-powered social annotation app for collecting and sharing trails of posts, links, and notes as guided walks through ideas and conversations.
classificationReason: Sidetrail is a user-facing app focused on creating and sharing annotation-style trails, so it fits best as an app-root listing under apps/sidetrail with an annotation discovery tag.

name: Yoten
tagline: A social tracker for your language learning journey.
fullDescription: Yoten is a social platform for tracking language-learning progress on AT Protocol. It lets you log study sessions and explore a Study Feed showing what friends and the global community are learning.
classificationReason: Consumer AT Protocol app centered on social language-learning session tracking and discovery.

name: Monomarks
tagline: Link-sharing for internet archaeologists
fullDescription: Monomarks is a social bookmarking app for collecting, organizing, and sharing links, with public collections and discovery aimed at internet archaeology.
classificationReason: Allows users to save and share curated link collections.

name: lexicons.bio
tagline: AT Protocol lexicons for decentralized biodiversity observation data.
fullDescription: lexicons.bio publishes AT Protocol lexicons for decentralized biodiversity observation data, with schemas aligned to Darwin Core Data Package and GBIF standards. It defines shared record formats for observations, associated media, and taxonomic identifications so biodiversity apps can exchange and interpret field data consistently across the AT Protocol ecosystem.
classificationReason: lexicons.bio is specifically focused on defining and publishing reusable AT Protocol lexicon schemas for biodiversity data, so it fits the protocol/lexicon category.

name: checkmate.blue
tagline: Chess on the Atmosphere
fullDescription: checkmate.blue is a chess game built for the Atmosphere network, offering a simple way to play chess in an AT Protocol-native context.
classificationReason: checkmate.blue is a consumer game with its own product identity focused on playing chess on the Atmosphere network, so it fits best as an app-root listing under apps/checkmate-blue with games tagging.

name: dndx
tagline: Searchable D&D compendium and campaign toolkit.
fullDescription: dndx is a web-based Dungeons & Dragons reference and campaign utility with searchable compendium content plus browse and tools sections for quick in-session lookup.
classificationReason: Community D&D web app centered on compendium search, browse, and gameplay support tools.

name: Skywell
tagline: A file sharing service built for AT Protocol.
fullDescription: Skywell is a file sharing service for AT Protocol that lets people upload and share files in a decentralized social ecosystem.
classificationReason: Skywell is an AT Protocol-native file sharing app and the requested category is apps/skywell.

name: PluralHost
tagline: Plural identity on the ATmosphere.
fullDescription: PluralHost helps plural systems manage member profiles and front-tracking on AT Protocol, with encrypted private data stored on the user’s own PDS and bidirectional sync with PluralKit.
classificationReason: PluralHost is an end-user AT Protocol application for managing system-member identity and social presence, so it fits app-level social tooling.

name: sparklepets
tagline: A cute and cozy virtual world on the Atmosphere.
fullDescription: Sparklepets is a cute and cozy virtual world powered by the Atmosphere where you adopt a little pal, care for them, and explore together.
classificationReason: Sparklepets is a standalone consumer game/virtual-world experience built on the Atmosphere, so it fits best as an app-root listing under apps/sparklepets.

name: Pocketenv
tagline: Experiment with AI tools, prompts, and agents in a private sandbox.
fullDescription: Pocketenv lets you experiment with AI tools, prompts, and agents in a private sandbox with no setup and no risk, where everything runs in a secure temporary environment that disappears when you're done.
classificationReason: Pocketenv is a developer-facing sandbox service for safely running AI experiments, prompts, and agents in isolated ephemeral environments.

name: Currents
tagline: Save inspiration and curate personalized visual feeds.
fullDescription: Currents helps you save inspiration from anywhere, organize collections around your taste, and tune your feed to discover more of what you love.
classificationReason: Currents is a consumer app experience focused on collecting and exploring visual inspiration feeds.

name: inspo.land
tagline: Photo inspiration for your next idea.
fullDescription: inspo.land is a photo inspiration app for browsing visual ideas and collecting references for creative projects.
classificationReason: User provided category apps/inspo-land and photo tag, indicating a consumer photo inspiration app.

name: TitleGraph
tagline: The federated catalog standard for streaming television.
fullDescription: TitleGraph is an open, decentralized data schema built on the AT Protocol for streaming television catalogs and availability data. It defines lexicons that separate canonical media metadata from delivery and commercial offer data so networks can syndicate VOD, live broadcast, and geo-availability information interoperably across the AT Protocol ecosystem.
classificationReason: TitleGraph publishes reusable AT Protocol lexicons and schema conventions for streaming catalog and delivery metadata, so it fits protocol/lexicon.

name: Lexicon Store
tagline: ATProto lexicon schema registry
fullDescription: Discover and explore AT Protocol lexicon schemas in a verified registry for Bluesky and ATProto projects.
classificationReason: Indexes and serves ATProto lexicon schema definitions for discovery and validation.

name: Chive
tagline: Decentralized eprints on ATProto.
fullDescription: Chive is an open-alpha decentralized eprint service on ATProto for sharing research with data sovereignty and community governance.
classificationReason: Chive is an end-user AT Protocol app focused on publishing and discovering decentralized research preprints, matching the requested apps/chive category.

name: Pollen
tagline: A gentle place for things to spread.
fullDescription: Pollen is a decentralized social app in the ATmosphere for sharing GIFs, thoughts, and moments in a calmer, garden-themed feed experience.
classificationReason: Pollen is a standalone AT Protocol social app focused on sharing posts and media, so it fits best as an app-root listing at apps/pollen-place.

name: atbbs
tagline: Bulletin boards on the Atmosphere.
fullDescription: Build a community from your existing account with tightly curated, fully portable bulletin boards that are open by design.
classificationReason: atbbs is an AT Protocol application focused on community bulletin-board conversations and messaging-style interaction.

name: ATStore
tagline: Discover apps and protocol tools across the Atmosphere.
fullDescription: ATStore is a directory for Atmosphere and Bluesky ecosystem apps, protocol tools, and services, helping users discover products that work with one portable account across the AT Protocol network.
classificationReason: ATStore is a user-facing discovery directory that catalogs Atmosphere and Bluesky ecosystem apps/tools, so it fits a dedicated apps/atstore directory category.

name: ATProto Science
tagline: Open, researcher-owned infrastructure and community for publishing, sharing, and discussing science on ATProto.
fullDescription: ATProto Science is a community-driven initiative helping researchers publish, curate, share, and discuss research using ATProto-powered apps and interoperable decentralized infrastructure.
classificationReason: A dedicated ATProto app ecosystem/community hub for scientific collaboration and scholarly communication.

name: atproto.camp
tagline: A directory of ATProtocol user groups.
fullDescription: atproto.camp is a gathering point for ATProtocol communities organized by city, region, and shared interests, helping people find local groups and grow the open social web together.
classificationReason: A web app that indexes and links ATProtocol community groups for discovery.

name: Cirrus
tagline: The lightest PDS in the atmosphere.
fullDescription: Cirrus is a single-user AT Protocol personal data server that runs on Cloudflare Workers, pairing edge runtime, Durable Objects, and R2 storage so individuals can self-host their identity and data without managing traditional servers.
classificationReason: Cirrus is a Personal Data Server implementation for AT Protocol that lets users run and control their own account infrastructure, so it belongs in protocol/pds.

name: Cocoon
tagline: An atproto PDS written in Go
fullDescription: Cocoon is a highly experimental, self-hostable AT Protocol PDS implementation in Go, with Docker Compose deployment, SQLite or Postgres support, and optional S3 blob storage/backups.
classificationReason: Implements a Personal Data Server for the AT Protocol.

name: adenosine-pds
tagline: Simple, enthusiast-grade AT Protocol personal data server in Rust.
fullDescription: adenosine-pds is an experimental personal data server implementation for the AT Protocol and Bluesky ecosystem, built in Rust for small-world self-hosting and protocol experimentation. It supports core Lexicon and repo workflows, DID resolution, and server-to-server interactions, but is explicitly positioned as hobby-grade software rather than production hosting.
classificationReason: adenosine-pds is a self-hostable implementation of an AT Protocol personal data server intended for development and experimentation, so it belongs in the protocol/pds category.
